## What landed (all std-3, `GQ2.AdmissibleCount` builds green)

The blocker was that `deepClassesSubgroup ρ.ker`'s `.Normal` instance resolves at top level but **fails in the nested position** `card_equivHoms_of_exact` needs it (the `AbsGalQ2`/`GaloisGroup` `instances`-transparency clash). The fix — the "view-normalization brick" the handoff called for:

- **`card_equivHoms_quotient_ses`** — the inclusion/quotient SES count stated over a **plain fvar** subgroup `Deep : AddSubgroup A`. Over fvars, `Normal`/`AddCommGroup`/`Finite` resolve with no coercion, so the engine applies cleanly.
- **`card_equivHoms_deepSES`** — instantiates it at `A := H¹(N)`, `Deep := deepClassesSubgroup (ker ρ)`, passing the `conjModule` actions as **named instance args** so no resolution against the view-clashed types is ever attempted. Gives the SES multiplicativity.
- **`card_deepPart_sq_of_duality`** — the **f6 output**: chains `card_H1_eq_card_fam · card_admissibleFam_eq · card_equivHoms_deepSES` against `card_deepPart_eq_card_deepFam · card_deepFam_eq`, and `hduality` collapses the SES product to a square ⟹ **`#X₊² = #H¹(ℚ₂,V)`**.

So P-15f6's five steps (gate + sub/quotient actions + SES count + deep bridge + assembly) are all done, std-3, sorry-free. **What remains is not f6** — it's the three *inputs* to the capstone: `hduality` (f7, graded Hilbert duality), the `V^∨` regular-summand package (f8, `lemma_6_11`), and `hinf`/`hext` (banked). Full detail in [`docs/p15f-handoff.md`](docs/p15f-handoff.md) §7.

# Which of the 15 axioms can be proved, and at what cost

Method: for each leaf I checked (a) what the cited proof in `references/` actually consumes, (b) what the pinned Mathlib (`ec410d2`, 2026-06-12) and the vendored `ClassFieldTheory` package (`3565c75`) provide today (via `docs/mathlib-cft-survey.md` plus fresh greps), and (c) how much of the repo's own infrastructure (ContCoh, Kummer machinery, unit filtration, corestriction, Fox calculus) already covers the gap. Effort estimates are calibrated to *this project's demonstrated pace* (LLM-assisted, ~a "ticket" = days, not the traditional months).

## Quick wins

**B12 (`kummerClassK_surjective`) — the clear first target.** The discharge route is already recorded in the axiom's own docstring and everything it needs exists: injectivity is already proved in-repo (`Kummer.kummerClass_eq_zero_iff`), Mathlib has the infinite Galois correspondence (`FieldTheory/Galois/Infinite.lean`), and the repo has `QuadraticAdjoin.lean`. The proof: a continuous hom `χ : G_k → ℤ/2` has open kernel of index ≤ 2; Krull gives a quadratic `L = k(√a)` (completing the square, char 0); then `χ = [a]` because two homs to `ℤ/2` with the same kernel are equal. ~1 ticket.

**B2 (`cyclotomicCharacter_two_surjective`) — free either way.** It has **zero consumers** (the adversarial review verified this); deleting it is a census 15→14 with no math. If you'd rather prove it: Mathlib has `IsCyclotomicExtension.autEquivPow`, `cyclotomicCharacter.continuous`, and compactness of `Gal(ℚ̄/ℚ)`, so the clean route is *closed image + dense image*: finite-level surjectivity via `autEquivPow` ∘ `restrictNormalHom_surjective` gives density in `ℤ₂ˣ = lim (ZMod 2^k)ˣ`; the image of a compact group is closed. 1–2 tickets. Only worth proving if you also want to decompose B8's bundled surjectivity input.

**B7′ (`hilbertSymbol_dyadic`).** The repo's `hilbertSymbol` is defined by norm-form solvability, independent of the formula, so this is exactly Serre *CiA* III §1.2 Thm 1 — a finite 2-adic computation: (i) `u ∈ ℤ₂ˣ` is a square iff `u ≡ 1 (mod 8)` (Hensel; `Mathlib.NumberTheory.Padics.Hensel` suffices), (ii) case analysis of `z² = ax² + by²` solvability over `(α,β) mod 2 × (u,v) mod 8`, with `decide` on `ZMod 8` doing the finite part and the only fiddly step being the primitive-solution normalization when lifting. This is squarely the kind of grind the repo has repeatedly executed (GaussSigns, HilbertLedger). 2–4 weeks. Payoff is large: B7′ sits in the `prop_8_9` and §6 footprints.

## The local-arithmetic middle tier

These all want one shared piece of infrastructure the repo has so far avoided: **honest residue fields / integer rings for `IntermediateField ℚ_[2] ℚ̄₂`** (unit ball `{x : ‖x‖ ≤ 1}` is a DVR with finite residue field). I'd make that bridge its own ticket (~2–4 weeks): discreteness of the value group comes from the freshly proved `UnitNormIndex.norm_val` (‖x‖ ∈ 2^{(1/n)ℤ} via Galois norms — the c2c2 work, still uncommitted on disk), compactness of the unit ball from finite-dimensionality, and Mathlib has `IsLocalRing.ResidueField` plus (new since the survey was written, worth noting) **Krasner's lemma** (`Analysis/Normed/Field/Krasner.lean`).

**B13 (`dyadicUnitFiltration`).** With the bridge: uniformizer existence = discreteness; `‖2‖ = ‖π‖^e` definitional; the graded counts `#gr⁰ = 2^f − 1`, `#grⁱ = 2^f` are the elementary isos `U⁰/U¹ ≅ k̄ˣ`, `Uⁱ/Uⁱ⁺¹ ≅ k̄⁺` (Serre LF IV §2 Prop 6 — the proof really is one page). 3–6 weeks total including the bridge. Alternative: port from the CFT package's `IsNonarchimedeanLocalField` (it has `e`, `f`, `e_mul_f_eq_n` proved) — but its `ℚ_[p]` instance still carries sorries and its `ValuativeRel` vocabulary would need its own bridge to spectral norms, so DIY is probably no slower.

**B11b (`unramifiedQuadratic_units_are_norms`).** Serre LF V §2 Prop 3: residue-level norm surjectivity (Mathlib has it for finite fields: `GaloisField.unitsMap_norm_surjective`) + successive approximation up the filtration + completeness. The extra repo-specific step is deriving `f = 2` from the spectral hypothesis (equal value groups) — which needs `ef = n`, another output of the residue bridge. ~1–2 months. Note this generalizes the exact computation I just did for c2c2, so the two workstreams share lemmas.

**B10′ (`tameQuotient`) — existence half.** Build `ℚ₂^{ur} = ∪ℚ₂(μ_odd)` and `ℚ₂^{tame} = ∪ℚ₂^{ur}(2^{1/m})` inside ℚ̄₂, compute `Gal(tame/ℚ₂)` by explicit Kummer/cyclotomic action (Mathlib's `KummerExtension` API helps), match it to the repo's presented `T_tame`, and show `Gal(ℚ̄₂/ℚ₂^{tame})` is pro-2 (totally-wildly-ramified degrees; needs the e/f theory over the henselian tame field — the residue bridge again). 1–3 months. The **orientation clauses cannot be "proved" in isolation** — they are compatibilities with B5's bundle; once the unramified theory exists they should become *derivable from B5* (units are unramified norms ⟹ units ↦ inertia), i.e. B10′ relaxes back to plain B10 rather than disappearing.

**B9 (`evensKahn_dyadic`).** Deceptively tractable because the axiom is already the *post-translation* statement: SW classes never appear — it's two identities among explicit cochain constructions the repo has fully defined (`corH1`, the two-point-graph `evensNormH2`, Kummer cocycles). Degree-1 is the classical `cor[a] = [N a]` compatibility — a finite transfer-sum computation, ~2 weeks, and I'd do it first as a probe. Degree-2 is Evens' index-2 norm formula `N(1+x) = 1 + tr(x) + N(x)` (Evens proves it by explicit cochain manipulation — algebra, no topology) combined with binary-trace-form bookkeeping; it means exhibiting an explicit cobounding 1-cochain between explicit 2-cocycles. High variance: 1–3 months, but fully self-contained in ContCoh — no new Mathlib theory.

**B11a (norm criterion).** The easy direction (`b` a norm ⟹ cup vanishes) follows from B9's degree-1 clause + the projection formula + `res[a]|_{k(√a)} = 0` — tier-2 given the B9-deg-1 work. The hard direction is equivalent to `[kˣ : N(k(√a)ˣ)] = 2` plus H²-detection — i.e. local CFT for quadratic extensions. The index computation is a unit-filtration grind (unramified case ≈ the c2c2 argument; ramified case = Serre LF V §3-style graded analysis, genuinely several weeks); detecting non-vanishing in the repo's `H²` without the invariant map needs the quaternion-extension criterion. 2–4 months all told; I'd sequence it after B9-deg-1, B11b and B13.

## The CFT core — B5, B6, B7

These share one bottleneck: the invariant map / Brauer computation `H²(G_k, μ) ≅ ℚ/ℤ` and its consequences, which nobody has formalized. The vendored ClassFieldTheory package is the right upstream (it has Tate cohomology, corestriction with `cores_res`, `localInv : H²(ℤ/n, ℤ) ≅ ZMod n`, Herbrand partials, filtration-completeness machinery) but at the pinned commit `LocalCFT/` contains only `Continuity` + `Teichmuller`, with 18 sorries in the local-field layer and **no fundamental class or Artin map**. Recommend a periodic re-sync of the pin — this is the fastest-moving upstream.

- **B5**: one honest in-repo route exists because the bundle only demands reciprocity **over the single base ℚ₂**: define `rec` explicitly on `ℚ₂ˣ = 2^ℤ × ℤ₂ˣ` via Frobenius × inverse-cyclotomic, then `norm_reciprocity` for all abelian `L/ℚ₂` reduces to local Kronecker–Weber for ℚ₂ plus explicit norm groups of cyclotomic/unramified towers. Self-contained but 6+ months; elementary LKW proofs exist though none is formalization-tested. Otherwise: wait for upstream and pay a ~2–4 week bridge (their framework → `Field.absoluteGaloisGroup ℚ_[2]` + the repo's normalization).
- **B7**: dévissage (LES + Shapiro + cor/res, mostly present in-repo) reduces it to trivial 𝔽₂-coefficients, where `#H¹ = 8` is Kummer (provable) but `#H² = 2` is the Brauer core again. It falls out of whatever discharges B5/B6; not worth attacking alone.
- **B6** is the heaviest of all fifteen: all finite `k/ℚ₂`, all `n`, all finite modules, perfectness in three degree pairs, *in continuous cohomology*. Even given upstream finite-level duality, the limit-assembly and pairing-matching into `TateDualityG` is months. Keep last.

## Deep structure theory — B4, B3c

Two layers: (i) *arithmetic* — `G_ℚ₂(2)` is Demushkin of rank 3 (dim H¹ = 3 is provable from Mathlib padics; dim H² = 1 and cup perfectness are the CFT core again, though perfectness would come cheaply from B7′-as-theorem once dim H² ≤ 1 is known); (ii) *classification* — Labute's Theorem 8 at `d = 1`, ~15 pages of delicate filtration/lifting pro-2 group theory formalized nowhere. Layer (ii) is actually the most attractive *standalone spin-off* from this repo — pure combinatorial group theory, no analysis, and the repo already owns Fox calculus, word machinery, and the Demushkin definitions — but it's a 3–6 month project. B3c additionally bundles the dualizing-character-equals-cyclotomic content (Labute Prop 6 + duality), so it costs strictly more than B4.

## Keep as axioms — B1, B8

**B1** is the Jannsen–Wingberg structure theorem; there is no soft shortcut. In particular, generation of `G_ℚ₂` is genuinely global: the pro-2 Sylow subgroups of `G_ℚ₂` are *infinitely* generated (the fixed field of a Sylow has infinite degree, so `Lˣ/(Lˣ)²` blows up), which kills every Sylow-by-Sylow or Frattini-style argument, and "small" (finitely many extensions per degree, now reachable via Mathlib's Krasner) does not imply f.g. even for prosolvable groups. If any leaf stays an axiom forever, this is the defensible one.

**B8**'s faithful content is the Galois action on `π₁^{ét}(ℙ¹∖{0,1,∞})` — needing étale fundamental groups *and* Riemann existence, neither on Mathlib's horizon — and the repo's abstract-group form (automorphisms of the free pro-2 group with three prescribed peripheral cyclotomic conjugacies) has no known geometry-free proof; abstract existence of such automorphisms is Grothendieck–Teichmüller-adjacent. Keep, with its documented B2/B5 companion note.

## On calibration: no, and thanks for the catch

My numbers were **not** in this project's units, despite my claim that they were. I anchored on conventional solo-human-formalizer calibration (where "2–4 weeks" ≈ one expert formalizing a paper section) and applied only a partial discount. Measured against the actual demonstrated throughput — the entire paper plus its infrastructure in ~1 calendar week across 6 lanes, i.e. ~40 lane-days for ~126 files — "2–4 weeks for B7′" is off by roughly an order of magnitude. B7′ is one ticket's worth of content: Hensel for squares-mod-8 plus a `decide`-friendly finite case analysis.

The defensible unit is the **lane-session** (one agent, one scoped ticket, ~½–1 day, ~200–1500 lines). Recalibrated:

| Item | Lane-sessions | At 6-lane cadence |
|---|---|---|
| B12 | 4–7 | ~1 day |
| B7′ | 5–10 | 1–2 days |
| B13 (incl. residue bridge) | 10–20 | 2–4 days |
| B11b (after bridge) | 8–15 | 2–3 days |
| **15 → 10 axioms** | — | **~1–2 weeks**, not "2–3 months" |

Two things survive from my original framing, and they're why I don't think pure linear scaling holds either. First, the paper formalized in a week partly because it came with a complete, formalization-engineered proof script (App. D dependency certificate); the axioms are exactly the nodes with *no* script, where the references argue in a different vocabulary (valuation rings, residue fields, Brauer groups). This week's own history shows the serial bottleneck there is statement/route design with you in the loop — the B10′ escalation, the B11 split, the B12/B13 proposal docs, the c2c2 statement design — not the proving. Second, 6-lane parallelism compresses *portfolios*, not single-item latency: one axiom occupies 1–2 lanes until decomposed, and the repo's fat tails (the f2a DI-core route death, the f2b fork) argue for reading these as medians with ×2–3 tails. For the tier-4 items (B5, B6, B7, Demushkin) the qualitative claim stands in any units: they contain more new-theory mass than the paper's own argument, which was engineered to sit on top of them.
